behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (file/env merge -> validation -> CLI error formatting), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: unit-testing validator alone could miss a bug where loader drops nested path context and emits a generic key name. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: a Unit test of only schema validation would miss failures in merged-source precedence/context propagation. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text only requires file+env config handling and startup error text, not subprocess/HTTP/OS-specific primitives beyond normal local I/O; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI step exists.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader through startup with a temp config file missing `database.host` and no matching `TOOL_` env var; assert startup fails and message includes exact path `database.host` (not a flattened/partial key).

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is env-resolution + path-construction + loader wiring behavior across modules. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem/env is enough; no subprocess/network. MOCK-HIDE: mocking path resolution in isolation can hide wrong precedence where loader still reads `~/.config`. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit test of path helper alone misses integration bug where caller ignores helper output. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is strictly path selection relative to `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, with no external process/network dependency; Agentic/Workflow rejected due no model call.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp dir containing config, also place a conflicting config under `~/.config` test home; run loader and assert it reads from `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/.../config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: precedence failure appears when env parsing and file parsing are combined and merged. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess beyond process env needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking one source can hide wrong merge order. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit test of merge function with synthetic maps may pass while real env key normalization (`TOOL_DATABASE_HOST` -> `database.host`) breaks in loader wiring. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is source precedence logic, not OS-specific external behavior; Agentic/Workflow rejected due no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create config file with `database.host=filehost`, set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost`, run loader, assert effective config uses `envhost`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is parser error object -> loader error translation -> CLI output. REAL-INFRA: requires real YAML parser behavior but not subprocess/network. MOCK-HIDE: parser mocks would always provide expected fields and can hide missing/incorrect line extraction from real exceptions. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit test with fabricated exception misses mismatch in real parser exception shape/attributes. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is local parse and message formatting, not external process/network; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Write malformed YAML in temp config (known bad line), run loader, assert failure message includes the reported line number from real parser output.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in startup orchestration (existence check -> mkdir -> write defaults -> continue load). REAL-INFRA: local filesystem is required, but still within Integration scope (local FS). MOCK-HIDE: fake filesystem can hide real path/join/order bugs and write sequencing errors. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Unit test of `ensure_dir()` or `write_defaults()` separately misses bug where defaults are not written before subsequent load. REJECTION-GATE: System considered, but behavior text only requires local directory/file creation at startup (no subprocess/network/OS event watcher semantics); Agentic/Workflow rejected due no AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Start with empty temp home/config root where config dir is absent; run startup loader; assert directory is created, defaults file exists with expected baseline keys, and loader proceeds without fatal error.
